intended_use: ART-Plan's indicated target population is cancer patients for whom radiotherapy treatment has been prescribed. In this population, any patient for whom relevant modality imaging data is available. ART
intended_use_full: ART-Plan's indicated target population is cancer patients for whom radiotherapy treatment has been prescribed. In this population, any patient for whom relevant modality imaging data is available. ART-Plan is not intended to be used for patients less than 18 years of age. The indicated users are trained medical professionals including, but not limited to, radiotherapists, radiation oncologists, medical physicists, dosimetrists and medical professionals involved in the radiation therapy process. The indicated use environments are, but not limited to, hospitals, clinics and any health facility involved in radiation therapy.
ground_truth: Manual contours performed by medical experts for autosegmentation validation; commercial solutions for dose engine comparison
other_metrics: Dice Similarity Coefficient (DSC) mean ≥0.8 for autosegmentation; Hausdorff Distance 95th percentile (HD95) ≤5.75mm for autosegmentation; DVH parameters (PTV) <2% for synthetic-CT dosimetry; DVH Individual Pass Rates (PTV) >76.7%; Median Gamma Index 2%/2mm ≥92%; Median Gamma Index 3%/3mm ≥93.57% for synthetic-CT dosimetry; Jacobian Determinant 1±5% for synthetic-CT geometric validation; Relative differences on DVH parameters (PTV/OARs) ≤4.4% (Lungs ≤24.4%) for dose engine validation; Median Gamma Index 2%/2mm ≥86.3% and 3%/3mm ≥91.75% for dose engine validation; Non-regression testing with maximum tolerance margin of -5% relative error for DSC between versions
